A Hamptons Hotel · Overlooking the Harbor

The harbor, gracefully held.

A luxury boutique hotel within walking distance of Main Street in the historic village of Sag Harbor — where the fresh sea air, colorful sails, and white sandy beaches have welcomed travelers for generations.

A Word From The Inn

Where Sag Harbor's quieter rhythm still keeps time.

The Sag Harbor Inn was made for travelers who recognize that the best of the Hamptons is not always the loudest part. Each of our forty-five deluxe, spacious guest rooms has its own private balcony or terrace, tastefully decorated with 18th-century inspired furniture.

Fully renovated with quiet contemporary comforts, the Inn offers a complimentary breakfast, an outdoor pool, a centrally located elevator, and a vast array of local activities — all within walking distance of Main Street and the harbor's edge.

A welcome from the team

Sag Harbor · The Hamptons

Stay With Us

Forty-five rooms, each with a balcony.

Spacious, restful, and freshly renovated — every guest room opens to the air, whether by private terrace or balcony. Many overlook the harbor itself.

  • Harborview

    The Deluxe King

    A spacious king bed, your own private balcony, and a wide window onto the harbor — where the morning light arrives over the water and the day unfolds at its own pace.

    • King Bed
    • Private Balcony
    • Harbor View
    • Renovated
    Reserve this room

  • Family-Sized

    The Double Queen Suite

    Two queen beds for travelers who don't want to choose between space and proximity. Doors open onto a terrace; the harbor sits a short walk beyond.

    • Two Queens
    • Private Terrace
    • Sleeps 4
    • Refrigerator
    Reserve this room

  • Gather

    The Harborview Gallery

    For weddings, showers, family reunions, corporate retreats, and small formal gatherings. A bright room with a view of the harbor — and an outdoor pool area available, in season, as an open-air alternative.

    • Weddings
    • Meetings
    • Receptions
    • Showers
    Inquire about events

The Inn Experience

An afternoon by the fireplace, a morning by the pool, an evening on Main Street.

The bustling beaches and outdoor dining of summer. The cool, crisp days of fall and the local farmers' market. The picturesque, snow-covered streets of winter. Sag Harbor is an ideal year-round destination — and the Inn is its quiet, dependable center.

Plan Your Visit

Included In Your Stay

Quietly thoughtful, without the fuss.

A list of the things we provide — kept short on purpose. The rest is just being on the water.

  • Outdoor Pool

    Available seasonally, with a sun-drenched deck and Adirondack chairs on the lawn.

  • Complimentary Breakfast

    Continental — served fresh each morning. Yogurt, fruit, pastries, and our exceptional coffee.

  • Lobby Fireplace

    The favorite room in the house — for reading, late-arrival check-ins, and wintering visitors.

  • Private Balcony or Terrace

    Every guest room opens to the air. Many face the harbor and its colorful sails.

  • Free Parking

    Ample, on-site, and complimentary — a rarer thing in the Hamptons than it ought to be.

  • High-Speed Wi-Fi

    Free in-room throughout the property, with laptop-friendly workspaces in every room.

Inside the Inn.

Travelers' Voices

Words from recent guests.

  • ★ ★ ★ ★ ★
    Rated 5 out of 5.
    Beautiful, well-organized meeting room with a gorgeous water view. The staff was very professional, helpful, and attentive. Highly recommend this venue for any occasion.
  • ★ ★ ★ ★ ★
    Rated 5 out of 5.
    Absolutely lovely. So peaceful and serene. I slept so well — for me this is a rarity. The continental breakfast was very nice and the front desk team was so welcoming. Book a room — you will not be dissatisfied.
  • ★ ★ ★ ★ ★
    Rated 5 out of 5.
    Helpful and kind staff. Beautiful harbor views, quick walk into town, the place is spotless. The continental breakfast is fresh, wonderful, and plentiful. We love this place.

★ 4.0 of 5 · 176 reviews on Tripadvisor

Where We Are

The heart of the historic village.

45 West Water Street
Sag Harbor, New York 11963

A short walk from Main Street's restaurants, shops, galleries, and the Bay Street Theater — and a shorter one to the harbor itself.

  • 2 minWalk to Main Street & the harbor
  • 7 minSag Harbor Whaling & Historical Museum
  • 8 minBay Street Theater & Center for the Arts
  • 13 minSag Harbor Marine Park
  • 35 miNew Haven Airport · ferry crossing

Ready When You Are

The harbor is still here. Come find it.
